title: polemoniella micrantha
intro: Polemoniella micrantha, also known as dwarf gilia, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's medicinal properties have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Polemoniella micrantha include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
